title: Medication list extraction
description: Strict contract for medications transcribed from an uploaded medication list (discharge med list, pharmacy printout, patient-carried list). Each medication is emitted as a RUN of attribute entries in document order: a `medication_name` entry starts a new medication, and the dose/route/frequency/prn/prescriber entries that follow it belong to that medication until the next `medication_name`. field_key is a CLOSED enum (the intake convention) — an out-of-vocabulary key rejects the extraction. Per-field page/quote citations are OPTIONAL (the intake convention, never in the required array): welcome when the source text is clearly identifiable, and a missing citation never fails the extraction. Values are transcribed EXACTLY as printed — never normalized, converted, or inferred; a blank or illegible attribute is value null. The schema is the source of truth: raw VLM output that does not validate against this shape is rejected before anything is persisted.

fields.description: Attribute entries in document order. Start each medication with a medication_name entry; follow it with that medication's other attributes.

field_key.description: Which medication attribute this entry is. medication_name starts a new medication.

value.description: The attribute exactly as printed (e.g. "Metformin", "500 mg", "oral", "twice daily", "PRN" or "as needed", "Dr. A. Chen"), or null if blank/illegible. Never normalize, convert, or invent a value.

confidence.description: Model self-reported confidence in [0,1]. Advisory only.

page.description: Optional: the 1-based page number the value was read from, when clearly identifiable. Omit when unsure — never guess.

quote.description: Optional: a short verbatim quote of the source text the value was read from. Omit when unsure — never invent.
